2606.24526.title: AGORA: An Archive-Grounded Benchmark for Agentic Workplace Document Reasoning
2606.24526.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents that reason over documents rather than answer from parametric knowledge. We study archive-grounded reasoning: locating sparse evidence across a large, messy collection of workplace files, reconciling inconsistent terminology, units, and time conventions, and computing an answer. Existing benchmarks address only parts of this setting and none jointly stresses archive-groundedness, agentic exploration, and cross-domain coverage. We introduce Agora, a benchmark pairing 362 questions with eight domain collections of 9,664 authentic documents and 372M tokens, far exceeding any model's context window, so agents must explore deliberately rather than scan exhaustively. Agora is built by an agentic pipeline combining cross-document task synthesis, leakage-preventing obfuscation, and difficulty filtering. Evaluating eight models, we find the task far from solved: even the strongest reaches only 59.4% accuracy, with notable variation across domains.
2606.24526.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作推理文檔的代理，而不是僅僅依賴參數知識來回答問題。我們研究基於檔案的推理：在大量雜亂的工作檔案中定位稀疏證據，調和不一致的術語、單位和時間慣例，並計算出答案。現有基準僅涵蓋這一環境的部分內容，且沒有一個基準同時強調基於檔案的推理、代理探索和跨領域覆蓋。我們介紹了Agora，一個基準，將362個問題與八個領域的9,664份真實文檔和372M標記配對，遠超過任何模型的上下文窗口，因此代理必須有意識地探索，而不是徹底掃描。Agora是通過一個代理管道構建的，該管道結合了跨文檔任務合成、防洩漏的模糊處理和難度過濾。我們評估了八個模型，發現這項任務遠未解決：即使是最強的模型也僅達到59.4%的準確率，且在不同領域之間存在顯著變化。
2606.24526.author: Honglin Guo et.al.
2606.24526.authors: Honglin Guo, Qi Zhang, Yu Zhang, Weijie Li, Rui Zheng, Zhikai Lei, Qiyuan Peng, Zhiheng Xi, Tao Gui, Qi Zhang
